│              │                            │       │           │ concepts as costly and resource-heavy, and node 2 states the course     │
│              │                            │       │           │ focuses on LLM-based applications. Sentence 2 is also supported by      │
│              │                            │       │           │ node 2, which explicitly says the course revolves around making         │
│              │                            │       │           │ LLM-based applications, matching the stated practical focus.            │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Precision       │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because the relevant nodes in retrieval contexts      │
│              │                            │       │           │ are ranked first and second: node 1 directly explains, with "if I go    │
│              │                            │       │           │ straight and start teaching you fine tuning I will not be able to       │
│              │                            │       │           │ teach you well," why fine-tuning is avoided, and node 2 reinforces      │
│              │                            │       │           │ that the course focuses on "LLM based applications." The irrelevant     │
│              │                            │       │           │ node 3 is correctly placed lower because, although it mentions "Not     │
│              │                            │       │           │ retraining," it does not address the course-specific reason for         │
│              │                            │       │           │ avoiding fine-tuning or training from scratch.                          │
│        PASS  │ Answer Relevancy           │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because the response is fully on-topic and directly   │
│              │                            │       │           │ addresses why the course avoids fine-tuning and training LLMs from      │
│              │                            │       │           │ scratch, with no irrelevant content.                                    │
│        PASS  │ Faithfulness               │ 0.50  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.50 because the actual output adds unsupported claims:    │
│              │                            │       │           │ it says the course may not cover training foundation models from        │
│              │                            │       │           │ scratch or may need a separate session/instructor, and it also goes     │
│              │                            │       │           │ beyond the context by saying instructors explicitly avoid retraining    │
│              │                            │       │           │ because most parameters capture core NLU abilities like reasoning and   │
│              │                            │       │           │ input transformation. The context only mentions that few students may   │
│              │                            │       │           │ train foundation models from scratch and that industry retraining       │
│              │                            │       │           │ usually does not touch most parameters.                                 │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Relevancy       │ 0.79  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.79 because the context does answer the question with     │
│              │                            │       │           │ points like “if I go straight and start teaching you fine tuning I      │
│              │                            │       │           │ will not be able to teach you well” and “Not retraining. We don't       │
│              │                            │       │           │ touch most of the parameters of the model here,” but it also includes   │
│              │                            │       │           │ some irrelevant or incomplete fragments such as “Is this clear?” and    │
│              │                            │       │           │ “through which your LLM,” which lowers the overall relevance.           │
│        PASS  │ answer correctness [GEval] │ 0.70  │ 0.50      │ The response captures the high-level idea that the course focuses on    │
│              │                            │       │           │ LLM-based applications rather than retraining models, which aligns      │
│              │                            │       │           │ with the expected answer. However, it adds several unsupported          │
│              │                            │       │           │ details about prerequisite pre-training concepts, student               │
│              │                            │       │           │ comprehension, foundation-model demand, separate sessions, and          │
│              │                            │       │           │ parameter-level explanations that are not in the expected output. It    │
│              │                            │       │           │ also misses the explicit point that fine-tuning and training from       │
│              │                            │       │           │ scratch require massive data, compute, infrastructure, technical        │
│              │                            │       │           │ expertise, and cost.                                                    │
│        PASS  │ simple explanation [GEval] │ 0.80  │ 0.50      │ The response is clear and mostly student-friendly, with simple bullet   │
│              │                            │       │           │ points explaining that the course focuses on building LLM               │
│              │                            │       │           │ applications rather than retraining models. It also gives               │
│              │                            │       │           │ understandable reasons like prerequisite sequencing and limited         │
│              │                            │       │           │ practical need, though one bullet uses more technical language such     │
│              │                            │       │           │ as “pre-training concepts,” “foundation models,” and “model             │
│              │                            │       │           │ parameters,” which makes it slightly less simple than the easiest       │
│              │                            │       │           │ level.                                                                  │
│                                                                                                                                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                                                                                         │
│  ❌ test_case_60                                                                                                                        │
│  ├──   Input:              Compare MCP vs plugins for app/data connectivity and bundled skills.                                         │
│  │     Actual Output:      - App/data connectivity                                                                                      │
│  │                           - MCPs: are the actual connectors you use to pull data from multiple apps. Use multiple MCPs to bring      │
│  │                         in data from different sources.                                                                              │
│  │                           - Plugins: don’t themselves create connectivity — they bundle the MCP connectors. A plugin gives you       │
│  │                         those MCPs in one place so the team can access the same connectors without hunting them down.                │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - Bundled skills (analysis/workflow)                                                                         │
│  │                           - MCPs: focus on connectivity; they do not encapsulate the various analysis routines (skills).             │
│  │                           - Plugins: are prepackaged bundles of skills (plus MCPs, slash commands, subagents, etc.) so you get       │
│  │                         analysis commands, subagents and connectors together as a one‑click package you can share with the team.     │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - Practical point (from context)                                                                             │
│  │                           - Build MCPs to fetch data, build skills to analyze it, and package both into a plugin to keep             │
│  │                         everything centralized and easy to distribute. Note: the plugin contents “don’t stay in Excel,” so Excel     │
│  │                         workflows aren’t considered here.                                                                            │
│  │     Expected Output:    **MCP** is mainly for **app/data connectivity** — it connects tools like Stripe, FDA, or other apps to       │
│  │                         bring data in.                                                                                               │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         **Plugins** are a **bundle of MCPs + skills** together — a prepackaged set of connectors, analysis           │
│  │                         skills, subcommands, etc., all in one place.                                                                 │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         So:                                                                                                          │
│  │                         - **MCP = connector**                                                                                        │
│  │                         - **Plugin = combined package of connectors + skills**                                                       │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  └── Metrics                                                                                                                            │
│       Status ┃ Metric                     ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason                                                                  │
│      ━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Recall          │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because sentences 1-3 are fully supported by nodes    │
│              │                            │       │           │ 1-3 in retrieval context: sentence 1 matches MCP as app/data            │
│              │                            │       │           │ connectivity and a connector, sentence 2 matches Plugin as a bundled    │
│              │                            │       │           │ collection of MCPs plus skills, and sentence 3 directly restates the    │
│              │                            │       │           │ MCP = connector and Plugin = combined package distinction.              │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Precision       │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because the top three nodes in the retrieval          │
│              │                            │       │           │ contexts are all relevant and correctly prioritized. Node 1 directly    │
│              │                            │       │           │ supports the comparison by quoting "Plugin is just the collection of    │
│              │                            │       │           │ MCP and skills" and "prepackaged bundle of that combined skills,"       │
│              │                            │       │           │ node 2 reinforces MCP as app/data connectivity by quoting "you can      │
│              │                            │       │           │ use multiple MCPs to bring data from multiple apps," and node 3         │
│              │                            │       │           │ further confirms the bundled-plugins idea with "Plugin is just the      │
│              │                            │       │           │ collection of various MCPs and various skills that you can use          │
│              │                            │       │           │ together" and "you get everything at one place." Since there are no     │
│              │                            │       │           │ irrelevant nodes ranked above relevant ones, the ordering is already    │
│              │                            │       │           │ optimal.                                                                │
│        PASS  │ Answer Relevancy           │ 0.88  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.88 because the response mostly addresses the MCP vs.     │
│              │                            │       │           │ plugins comparison for app/data connectivity and bundled skills, but    │
│              │                            │       │           │ it includes some off-topic details about plugin content locations and   │
│              │                            │       │           │ Excel-specific workflows that don't support the core comparison. That   │
│              │                            │       │           │ keeps it relevant overall, just not perfectly focused.                  │
│        FAIL  │ Faithfulness               │ 0.40  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.40 because the actual output overstates what the         │
│              │                            │       │           │ context says about MCPs and plugins: it treats MCPs as real             │
│              │                            │       │           │ connectors that pull data from multiple apps and sources, and claims    │
│              │                            │       │           │ practical guidance about how to build them. The context only says       │
│              │                            │       │           │ plugins are collections of MCPs and skills, can include MCP             │
│              │                            │       │           │ connectors in a one-click instruction, and does not mention Excel or    │
│              │                            │       │           │ whether plugin contents stay in Excel.                                  │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Relevancy       │ 0.81  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.81 because most of the retrieved text is on-topic and    │
│              │                            │       │           │ explains that a plugin is "the collection of MCP and skills" and "a     │
│              │                            │       │           │ prepackaged bundle" for using "multiple apps" and "multiple MCPs"       │
│              │                            │       │           │ together, which supports the app/data connectivity comparison.          │
│              │                            │       │           │ However, some parts are still filler or scenario framing, such as       │
│              │                            │       │           │ "Let's say you are in the marketing team," "Forget marketing," and      │
│              │                            │       │           │ "So what you can do?", so the context is relevant but not perfectly     │
│              │                            │       │           │ focused.                                                                │
│        PASS  │ answer correctness [GEval] │ 0.90  │ 0.50      │ The response matches the core distinction: MCPs are described as        │
│              │                            │       │           │ connectors for app/data connectivity, and plugins are described as      │
│              │                            │       │           │ bundled packages combining MCPs with skills, subagents, and commands.   │
│              │                            │       │           │ It also preserves the summary that MCP = connector and plugin =         │
│              │                            │       │           │ combined package. The only slight issue is added context about Excel    │
│              │                            │       │           │ workflows that is not in the expected output, but it does not           │
│              │                            │       │           │ contradict the main meaning.                                            │
│        PASS  │ simple explanation [GEval] │ 0.80  │ 0.50      │ The response is easy to read and organized with bullets, and it         │
│              │                            │       │           │ clearly explains the contrast between app/data connectivity and         │
│              │                            │       │           │ bundled skills. However, it uses some technical terms like MCPs,        │
│              │                            │       │           │ subagents, and slash commands without simplifying them much, so it is   │
│              │                            │       │           │ only moderately simple rather than very simple or fully                 │
│              │                            │       │           │ student-friendly.                                                       │
│                                                                                                                                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                                                                                         │
│  ❌ test_case_61                                                                                                                        │
│  ├──   Input:              Show JSON for plugin w/ 1 MCP, 2 skills, 1 slash cmd, 1 subagent.                                            │
│  │     Actual Output:      {                                                                                                            │
│  │                           "plugin_id": "plugin_sample_01",                                                                           │
│  │                           "name": "Combined MCP & Skills Plugin",                                                                    │
│  │                           "version": "1.0.0",                                                                                        │
│  │                           "description": "Prepackaged bundle of MCP connectors, skills, slash commands, and subagents.",             │
│  │                           "mcps": [                                                                                                  │
│  │                             {                                                                                                        │
│  │                               "id": "mcp_snowflake",                                                                                 │
│  │                               "type": "connector",                                                                                   │
│  │                               "name": "Snowflake",                                                                                   │
│  │                               "description": "Snowflake connector; requires authentication via Connectors UI.",                      │
│  │                               "authentication_required": true,                                                                       │
│  │                               "auth_instructions": "Go to connectors, search for 'snowflake', and authenticate."                     │
│  │                             }                                                                                                        │
│  │                           ],                                                                                                         │
│  │                           "skills": [                                                                                                │
│  │                             {                                                                                                        │
│  │                               "id": "skill_full_analysis",                                                                           │
│  │                               "name": "Full Analysis",                                                                               │
│  │                               "description": "Retrieves full data from Snowflake and performs complete analysis.",                   │
│  │                               "uses_mcp": [                                                                                          │
│  │                                 "mcp_snowflake"                                                                                      │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "inputs": [                                                                                            │
│  │                                 "query",                                                                                             │
│  │                                 "schema",                                                                                            │
│  │                                 "table"                                                                                              │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "outputs": [                                                                                           │
│  │                                 "data",                                                                                              │
│  │                                 "analysis_report"                                                                                    │
│  │                               ]                                                                                                      │
│  │                             },                                                                                                       │
│  │                             {                                                                                                        │
│  │                               "id": "skill_summary",                                                                                 │
│  │                               "name": "Summary Analysis",                                                                            │
│  │                               "description": "Generates a concise summary of data analysis results.",                                │
│  │                               "uses_mcp": [                                                                                          │
│  │                                 "mcp_snowflake"                                                                                      │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "inputs": [                                                                                            │
│  │                                 "data"                                                                                               │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "outputs": [                                                                                           │
│  │                                 "summary"                                                                                            │
│  │                               ]                                                                                                      │
│  │                             }                                                                                                        │
│  │                           ],                                                                                                         │
│  │                           "slash_commands": [                                                                                        │
│  │                             {                                                                                                        │
│  │                               "command": "/analyze",                                                                                 │
│  │                               "description": "Invoke analysis skills on specified Snowflake data.",                                  │
│  │                               "triggers": [                                                                                          │
│  │                                 "skill_full_analysis",                                                                               │
│  │                                 "skill_summary"                                                                                      │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "usage": "/analyze <query>"                                                                            │
│  │                             }                                                                                                        │
│  │                           ],                                                                                                         │
│  │                           "subagents": [                                                                                             │
│  │                             {                                                                                                        │
│  │                               "id": "subagent_auth_and_fetch",                                                                       │
│  │                               "name": "Auth and Fetch Subagent",                                                                     │
│  │                               "description": "Handles connector authentication and data fetching from Snowflake for skills.",        │
│  │                               "responsibilities": [                                                                                  │
│  │                                 "authenticate_connector",                                                                            │
│  │                                 "execute_query",                                                                                     │
│  │                                 "return_data"                                                                                        │
│  │                               ],                                                                                                     │
│  │                               "used_by": [                                                                                           │
│  │                                 "skill_full_analysis",                                                                               │
│  │                                 "skill_summary"                                                                                      │
│  │                               ]                                                                                                      │
│  │                             }                                                                                                        │
│  │                           ]                                                                                                          │
│  │                         }                                                                                                            │
│  │     Expected Output:    A plugin is a prepackaged bundle that can combine:                                                           │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - 1 MCP connector                                                                                            │
│  │                         - 2 skills                                                                                                   │
│  │                         - 1 slash command                                                                                            │
│  │                         - 1 subagent                                                                                                 │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         So in JSON, it can look like this:                                                                           │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         ```json                                                                                                      │
│  │                         {                                                                                                            │
│  │                           "plugin": {                                                                                                │
│  │                             "name": "sample-plugin",                                                                                 │
│  │                             "mcp_connectors": [                                                                                      │
│  │                               {                                                                                                      │
│  │                                 "name": "sample-mcp",                                                                                │
│  │                                 "type": "connector"                                                                                  │
│  │                               }                                                                                                      │
│  │                             ],                                                                                                       │
│  │                             "skills": [                                                                                              │
│  │                               {                                                                                                      │
│  │                                 "name": "skill-1",                                                                                   │
│  │                                 "type": "skill"                                                                                      │
│  │                               },                                                                                                     │
│  │                               {                                                                                                      │
│  │                                 "name": "skill-2",                                                                                   │
│  │                                 "type": "skill"                                                                                      │
│  │                               }                                                                                                      │
│  │                             ],                                                                                                       │
│  │                             "slash_commands": [                                                                                      │
│  │                               {                                                                                                      │
│  │                                 "name": "/sample-command",                                                                           │
│  │                                 "type": "slash_command"                                                                              │
│  │                               }                                                                                                      │
│  │                             ],                                                                                                       │
│  │                             "subagents": [                                                                                           │
│  │                               {                                                                                                      │
│  │                                 "name": "sample-subagent",                                                                           │
│  │                                 "type": "subagent"                                                                                   │
│  │                               }                                                                                                      │
│  │                             ]                                                                                                        │
│  │                           }                                                                                                          │
│  │                         }                                                                                                            │
│  │                         ```                                                                                                          │
│  └── Metrics                                                                                                                            │
│       Status ┃ Metric                     ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason                                                                  │
│      ━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Recall          │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because sentence 1 is fully supported by node 1 in    │
│              │                            │       │           │ retrieval context, which states that a plugin is a prepackaged          │
│              │                            │       │           │ bundle; sentence 2 is supported by node 1 and node 2, which together    │
│              │                            │       │           │ cover the included MCP connector(s), skills, slash command(s), and      │
│              │                            │       │           │ subagent(s).                                                            │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Precision       │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because the retrieval contexts are perfectly          │
│              │                            │       │           │ ordered: the 1st node in retrieval contexts directly supports the       │
│              │                            │       │           │ full plugin structure with "MCP connectors, set, slash commands,        │
│              │                            │       │           │ subagents" and "Plugin is just the collection of various MCPs and       │
│              │                            │       │           │ various skills," the 2nd node reinforces the need for "various          │
│              │                            │       │           │ skills" to match the 2 skills, and the 3rd node supports the MCP        │
│              │                            │       │           │ connector with "how to use MCP inside plugin" and "snowflake            │
│              │                            │       │           │ connector." There are no irrelevant nodes placed above relevant ones,   │
│              │                            │       │           │ so nothing needed to be ranked lower.                                   │
│        PASS  │ Answer Relevancy           │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00 because the output is fully aligned with the          │
│              │                            │       │           │ request and contains no irrelevant statements; it directly provides     │
│              │                            │       │           │ the requested JSON structure for the plugin with 1 MCP, 2 skills, 1     │
│              │                            │       │           │ slash command, and 1 subagent.                                          │
│        FAIL  │ Faithfulness               │ 0.17  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.17 because the actual output invents many specifics      │
│              │                            │       │           │ not supported by the retrieval context: a plugin_id, plugin name and    │
│              │                            │       │           │ version, a Snowflake connector id like "mcp_snowflake", an              │
│              │                            │       │           │ "authentication_required" field, skills such as "Full Analysis" and     │
│              │                            │       │           │ "Summary Analysis" with ids like "skill_full_analysis" and              │
│              │                            │       │           │ "skill_summary", their inputs/outputs, a "/analyze" slash command and   │
│              │                            │       │           │ its syntax, and a subagent "subagent_auth_and_fetch" with listed        │
│              │                            │       │           │ responsibilities. The context only generally mentions connectors,       │
│              │                            │       │           │ skills, and authentication, so these details are not grounded in it.    │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Relevancy       │ 0.50  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.50 because the context only broadly matches the          │
│              │                            │       │           │ request: it says a plugin is a “collection of MCP and skills” and       │
│              │                            │       │           │ mentions “MCP connectors, set, slash commands, subagents,” which        │
│              │                            │       │           │ supports the asked components. However, much of it is still vague or    │
│              │                            │       │           │ irrelevant, like “Plugin is nothing.” and “You can do everything.”,     │
│              │                            │       │           │ so it doesn’t fully address the specific “1 MCP, 2 skills, 1 slash      │
│              │                            │       │           │ cmd, 1 subagent” JSON request.                                          │
│        FAIL  │ answer correctness [GEval] │ 0.40  │ 0.50      │ The response is structurally similar but not an exact or logically      │
│              │                            │       │           │ equivalent match to the expected plugin JSON. The expected output       │
│              │                            │       │           │ specifies 1 MCP connector, 2 skills, 1 slash command, and 1 subagent    │
│              │                            │       │           │ with simple name/type fields under a top-level "plugin" object, while   │
│              │                            │       │           │ the actual output uses different keys and extra metadata such as        │
│              │                            │       │           │ plugin_id, version, descriptions, authentication details, and nested    │
│              │                            │       │           │ IDs. It also omits the required top-level "plugin" wrapper and          │
│              │                            │       │           │ changes field names like "mcp_connectors" to "mcps".                    │
│        PASS  │ simple explanation [GEval] │ 0.70  │ 0.50      │ The output is valid JSON and matches the requested structure with 1     │
│              │                            │       │           │ MCP, 2 skills, 1 slash command, and 1 subagent. However, it is fairly   │
│              │                            │       │           │ technical, using terms like MCP, connector authentication, triggers,    │
│              │                            │       │           │ and responsibilities, so it is not especially student friendly or       │
│              │                            │       │           │ very simple.                                                            │
│                                                                                                                                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                                                                                         │
│  ❌ test_case_62                                                                                                                        │
│  ├──   Input:              How does MCP differ from plugins in Excel for connectors, auth, app integration, and limits?                 │
│  │     Actual Output:      - Connectors                                                                                                 │
│  │                           - MCP: Provides connectors that you can use inside Excel to pull data from multiple apps; MCPs can be      │
│  │                         used together to bring data from different apps and can apply rules across multiple Excel files in a         │
│  │                         directory.                                                                                                   │
│  │                           - Plugin: A prepackaged bundle that includes MCP connectors (and skills); plugins are hosted (e.g., on     │
│  │                         Entropic) for reuse but, per the context, “don’t stay in Excel,” so they aren’t treated as in-Excel          │
│  │                         connectors.                                                                                                  │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - Auth                                                                                                       │
│  │                           - I don't know. The context does not describe how authentication is handled for MCPs or plugins.           │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - App integration                                                                                            │
│  │                           - MCP: Used inside Excel to integrate data from multiple apps by combining multiple MCPs/connectors.       │
│  │                           - Plugin: Packages MCPs, skills, slash commands, subagents, etc., into a one-click bundle for              │
│  │                         distribution and reuse across users/apps, but is external to Excel.                                          │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - Limits (usage, size, scope, etc.)                                                                          │
│  │                           - I don't know. The context does not specify any limits for MCPs or plugins.                               │
│  │     Expected Output:    MCP and plugins are different in purpose:                                                                    │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         - **MCP** is a **connector/protocol** used to connect apps and bring data from multiple sources into         │
│  │                         Excel or Claude.                                                                                             │
│  │                         - **Plugins** are a **prepackaged bundle** of **MCP connectors + skills** in one place.                      │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         Key differences:                                                                                             │
│  │                         - **Connectors:** MCP is the connector layer; plugins include connectors inside them.                        │
│  │                         - **Auth:** MCP connectors usually need separate authentication; plugins handle the setup for you within     │
│  │                         the bundle.                                                                                                  │
│  │                         - **App integration:** MCP focuses on linking specific apps; plugins combine multiple app connectors and     │
│  │                         skills together.                                                                                             │
│  │                         - **Limits:** MCP is more about direct integration; plugins are limited to the skills/connectors included    │
│  │                         in that plugin.                                                                                              │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         So, **MCP connects**, while **plugins package connectors and skills together for easier use**.               │
│  └── Metrics                                                                                                                            │
│       Status ┃ Metric                     ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason                                                                  │

│                                                                                                                                         │
│  ❌ test_case_63                                                                                                                        │
│  ├──   Input:              What 3 question-paper setup approaches for RAG eval datasets, plus pros/cons, are mentioned?                 │
│  │     Actual Output:      1) Manual / human-authored question papers                                                                   │
│  │                            - What context says: you must create multiple exams across multiple subjects.                             │
│  │                            - Con(s) (explicit): high manual effort / scalability problem.                                            │
│  │                            - Pro(s): not specified in the provided context.                                                          │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         2) Historical data (previous-year question papers)                                                           │
│  │                            - What context says: reuse existing past exam papers — "previous year question paper is already there     │
│  │                         I can catch them and fetch some questions and make paper."                                                   │
│  │                            - Pro(s) (explicit): can be reused directly to assemble papers (reduces need to author from scratch).     │
│  │                            - Con(s): not specified in the provided context.                                                          │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         3) Synthetic data (generated Q&A pairs)                                                                      │
│  │                            - What context says: generate question–answer pairs (e.g., via frameworks like DeepEval) as a "key"       │
│  │                         for RAG evaluation; used to set question papers programmatically.                                            │
│  │                            - Pro(s) (explicit): enables generation of many Q/A pairs (helps with scale and reducing manual           │
│  │                         effort); integrates with RAG evaluation tooling.                                                             │
│  │                            - Con(s): not specified in the provided context.                                                          │
│  │     Expected Output:    The 3 question-paper setup approaches are:                                                                   │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         1. **Manual creation**                                                                                       │
│  │                            - **Pros:** Human control, can ensure coverage and balanced difficulty.                                   │
│  │                            - **Cons:** Biased, costly, hard to scale, depends on human availability, and breaks when the             │
│  │                         knowledge base updates.                                                                                      │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         2. **Historical data**                                                                                       │
│  │                            - **Pros:** Uses real previous user questions, so it reflects actual usage.                               │
│  │                            - **Cons:** May have low coverage, still needs correct answers for scoring, and can be limited by one     │
│  │                         type of user behavior.                                                                                       │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         3. **Synthetic data**                                                                                        │
│  │                            - **Pros:** Automated, scalable, lower cost, less bias, no fatigue/illness issues, and adapts better      │
│  │                         to knowledge base updates.                                                                                   │
│  │                            - **Cons:** Needs a smart generation method, which was only briefly mentioned in the context.             │
│  └── Metrics                                                                                                                            │
│       Status ┃ Metric                     ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason                                                                  │

│                                                                                                                                         │
│  ❌ test_case_64                                                                                                                        │
│  ├──   Input:              Synthetic Q-A gen for eval: pros, cons, and required JSON fields?                                            │
│  │     Actual Output:      Pros:                                                                                                        │
│  │                         - Works when you don't have your real dataset available.                                                     │
│  │                         - Useful for RAG scenarios (e.g., transcripts) where reading full context and creating Q‑As manually is      │
│  │                         difficult.                                                                                                   │
│  │                         - Gives a fast starting point for evaluation and development.                                                │
│  │                         - Can be automated to generate many examples quickly (though automation may not always be appropriate).      │
│  │                         - You can start with a small synthetic pool (e.g., 50–100) and filter down to the best examples.             │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         Cons:                                                                                                        │
│  │                         - Quality varies — not all synthetic data is equally reliable.                                               │
│  │                         - Inferior to a manually created dataset; manual is the best approach when possible.                         │
│  │                         - Not safe for high‑stakes workflows unless reviewed: you must review, edit, and enrich generated            │
│  │                         examples before treating them as ground truth.                                                               │
│  │                         - Risk of being “greedy” (generate too many low‑quality examples); guideline: use less but use the best.     │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         Required JSON fields:                                                                                        │
│  │                         I don't know.                                                                                                │
│  │     Expected Output:    Synthetic Q-A generation for evaluation is a **smart, automated approach** to create your question paper     │
│  │                         from the knowledge base.                                                                                     │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         **Pros:**                                                                                                    │
│  │                         - **No human bias**                                                                                          │
│  │                         - **Better scalability**                                                                                     │
│  │                         - **Lower cost**                                                                                             │
│  │                         - **Easy to regenerate when the knowledge base updates**                                                     │
│  │                         - **Avoids fatigue/availability issues** of manual creation                                                  │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         **Cons:**                                                                                                    │
│  │                         - You still need to ensure the generated questions have **good coverage**                                    │
│  │                         - The generated answers must be **grounded in the knowledge base**                                           │
│  │                         - Quality depends on the synthetic generation method                                                         │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         **Required JSON fields:**                                                                                    │
│  │                         - **question**                                                                                               │
│  │                         - **answer**                                                                                                 │
│  │                         - **metadata/source info** (to ensure it comes from the knowledge base)                                      │
│  │                                                                                                                                      │
│  │                         If you want, I can also give this in a more concrete **DPVAL-style JSON schema**.                            │
│  └── Metrics                                                                                                                            │
│       Status ┃ Metric                     ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason                                                                  │
│      ━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
